terms.definition: The projection of inner human intentions into external artefacts, symbols or systems
A branch of philosophy concerned with what types of things exist and how they relate
The strength and stability of cooperative social bonds within a population
Collective trust that communicated information is accurate, non-manipulative, and verifiable
A state of heightened cognitive and emotional disorder driven by information overload and social comparison
In thermodynamics, the measure of unavailable energy; metaphorically, the tendency toward systemic disorder
An industrial design strategy that limits product lifespan to stimulate repurchase cycles
An analytical framework that treats technology as value-laden yet democratically steerable
A historically bounded period characterised by a dominant communication or interaction topology
A design ethos that normalises disposability and systemic instability as stylistic virtues
